files.content:
from flask import Flask, request, render_template_string, escape from app.models import SearchResult from app.services.search_engine import perform_search app = Flask(__name__) @app.route('/search') def search(): query = request.args.get('q', '') page = request.args.get('page', '1') results = perform_search(query, int(page)) # Developer used escape() on the query but forgot to escape result snippets # which come from user-generated content in the database template = '''

Search results for: {{ query }}

{% for result in results %}
{{ result.title }}

''' + '{{ result.snippet|safe }}' + '''

{% endfor %}
Next
''' return render_template_string( template, query=query, results=results, next_page=int(page) + 1 )

issues.description: Result snippet marked as |safe in Jinja2 template, bypassing auto-escaping. Since snippets originate from user-generated database content, this enables stored XSS

Using render_template_string with dynamically constructed template string. While Jinja2 auto-escaping protects most variables, the |safe filter on line 24 and the string concatenation pattern create injection risk
